stemHtml: What is the General Concept of the NAT in-flight contingency procedures? Whenever operationally feasible…

options.a: To offset 5 NM left or right of the cleared route and descend below FL290, then maintain a 500 ft vertical offset until an ATC clearance received. If not descending below FL290, establish a 500 ft vertical offset.
options.b: To offset 15 NM left or right of the cleared route and descend below FL290, then maintain a 500 ft vertical offset until an ATC clearance received. If not descending below FL290, establish a 500 ft vertical offset.
options.c: To offset 5 NM left or right of the cleared route and descend below FL290, then maintain a 300 ft vertical offset until an ATC clearance received. If not descending below FL290, establish a 300 ft vertical offset.
options.d: To offset 5 NM left or right of the cleared route and descend below FL290, then descend 300 ft if right of track and climb 300 ft if left of track, maintain offset until an ATC clearance received.
